evidence: TASK-036 (FEAT-006 T3, deterministic 3-way schema merge) verified. Commit 39d0458: new schema-merge.ts mergeSchema(base, ours, theirs) -> {merged, conflicts} (pure; canonicalJson equality; MISSING unique-symbol sentinel) implements the recursive 3-way: all-three-objects per-key over the sorted union (MISSING-merged keys omitted = deletion); SET-ARRAY (last segment required/enum/type, all sides arrays) = (ours∩theirs)∪(ours\base)∪(theirs\base) sorted; add/remove (one side MISSING) present-on-one->take, both-add-differing->conflict, removed-vs-unchanged->honor, delete-vs-modify->conflict; atomic 3-way for scalars/non-set-arrays/kind-mismatch (all-differ -> {path,base,ours,theirs} conflict). Wired into updateContext's locally-modified/both-diverged arm: base via getObject(content_hash) (null -> refused fallback), ours=installed, theirs=catalog; conflict-free -> writeSchemaCheckedForDir(meta-validate + nested-id guard + atomic write; dryRun validates-only) + result.merged; conflicts -> result.conflicts (no write); throw -> refused; brought_current extended with result.merged so an auto-merged body is re-base-stamped as the next base. UpdateResult gains merged/conflicts; the update op unchanged. Tests: schema-merge.test.ts 15/15; install-subcommand merge cases (d) disjoint auto-merge both survive, (e) same-node conflict bytes-unchanged, (f) enum widening set-union, (g) delete-vs-modify conflict, (h) merged re-baselined+stamped — all pass on real substrates. build+check+test green (pi-context 1066 pass, fail 0). Fresh adversarial probe: PASS, no defects (own merge harness 6/6 + real-updateContext harness 13/13; set-array gated on all-arrays; null vs MISSING distinguished; allOf -> atomic; dryRun/conflict write nothing; changed tests strengthened not weakened). Documented conservatism (not a defect): when the base lacks a set field entirely and both sides add it with different arrays, it is recorded as a conflict rather than set-unioned (safe-conservative; surfaces the divergence). The conflict resolver consuming result.conflicts is TASK-037.

criteria_results.criterion: mergeSchema returns {merged, conflicts[]}: disjoint key additions merge; required/enum/type-union arrays merge as sets; overlapping edits to the same node emit a typed {path,base,ours,theirs} conflict
the merged output passes AJV draft-07 meta-validation + assertNoNestedIdBearingArray before any write
